title: repositories file inside tar file is empty after skopeo copy with docker-archive
body:
I am running the skopeo command as follow:
(Example command)
`skopeo copy --src-tls-verify=false  docker://marketplace.io/busybox:latest docker-archive:tmp1/busybox_latest.tar`

This will download the docker image `busybox:latest` and save it in the tar file `busybox_latest.tar`. We are downloading the image from Harbor registry.

Now when I do `docker load < busybox_latest.tar` it loads the image with name and tag as `<none>` (Checked it using docker command `docker images -a`)

Am I missing passing any parameter to skopeo copy command?

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

Yes; `skopeo copy` treats the source and destination as completely independent, it does not transfer anything from the source name to the destination. Try something like `docker-archive:path.tar:marketplace.io/busybox:latest`.

@mtrmac Thanks for the response. I already figured out how to use docker-archive in the copy command from one of your unit tests. We should add this in the `skopeo copy` documentation on how to use skopeo copy command with docker-archive.

The documentation already says https://github.com/containers/skopeo/blob/e4297e3b3051346ad6727198319b86af92436fe9/docs/skopeo-copy.1.md?plain=1#L18-L19 ; can that be much cleaner?

I suppose adding another example at the end wouldn’t hurt, and might help.
Another example would be very helpful. Also, docker-archive example would be more helpful.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
